content: Hi, I'm Sutton, a DevOps Engineer at a cloud platform. I'm working remotely from my home in Denver, CO, and trying to establish a better daily routine now that I've adopted a parrot named Milo from a shelter.
Congratulations on the new feathered family member! How's the adjustment been so far?
It's been great, but also a bit challenging. I have to wake up earlier now to feed Milo and clean his cage. I'm usually up by 7:00 AM.
That's wonderful! It's great that you're prioritizing Milo's needs. How do you balance your work and personal life now?
I try to schedule my work tasks around Milo's feeding and playtime. I also love working on home automation projects and skiing in my free time.
That sounds like a great way to manage your time. What kind of home automation projects do you enjoy working on?
I love working on smart lighting systems and security cameras. It's so much fun to see everything come together and work seamlessly.

That's really cool! Home automation can be a wonderful way to make your life more convenient and efficient. Have you thought about how you'll continue to balance your work and personal life as Milo grows up?
Yeah, I've been thinking about that. I might need to hire a pet sitter or ask a friend to help out sometimes. My friend, Jack, has been really helpful with Milo so far.
It's great that you have a supportive friend. Let's work on creating a plan for your daily routine and Milo's care.
I've also been thinking about attending a conference on rust programming on November 20th. I've been learning a lot about it lately, and I think it could be a great way to improve my skills.
That's a great idea! Attending conferences can be a wonderful way to learn from experts and connect with other professionals in the field.
